questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Calculate the energy required to excite one litre of hydrogen gas at 1 atm and 298 K to the first excited state of atomic hydrogen. The energy for the dissociation of $\mathrm{H}-\mathrm{H}$ bond is $436 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$. (mark the answer in KJ)
questions.solution:
For n moles of an ideal gas $\mathrm{PV}=\mathrm{nRT}, \mathrm{n}=\frac{\mathrm{PV}}{\mathrm{RT}}=\frac{1 \times 1}{0.0821 \times 298}=0.0409$
Energy required to effect dissociation of 0.0409 mole of hydrogen $=436 \times 0.0409=17.83 \mathrm{~kJ}$.
Energy required to excite one hydrogen atom to the first excited state $=13.6\left(1-\frac{1}{4}\right)$
$=10.2 \mathrm{eV}=10.2 \times 1.6 \times 10^{-19} \mathrm{~kJ}=1.632 \times 10^{-21} \mathrm{~kJ}$
No. of H-atoms in the sample $=0.0409 \times 2 \times 6.02 \times 10^{23}$
So energy required for the excitation $=1.632 \times 10^{-21} \times 0.0409 \times 2 \times 6.02 \times 10^{23}=80.36 \mathrm{~kJ}$ The energy required $=17.83+80.36=98.19 \mathrm{~kJ}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The electron in the ground state for a H -atom absorbs a photon of wavelength 97.20 nm and it reaches to maximum level ' $n$ ' then when it returns from this level to ground state. Maximum numbers of lines can be obtained in its spectrum?
questions.solution: Electron will reach $n=4$, so the maximum possible transitions for single electron are $4 \rightarrow 3$, $3 \rightarrow 2,2 \rightarrow 1$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: 25 ml of 11.2 volume $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$ solution is titrated with $25 \mathrm{ml}_{\mathrm{KMnO}_4 / \mathrm{H}^{+}}$. If 1 L same $\mathrm{KMnO}_4$ is used to oxidize 1 mole of an element $X$ so that it is converted into a single species in which ' $X$ ' contains a single electron. If this single species is irradiated with light radiation of $114 Å$ so that electron is excited into a particular energy level. Determine no. of transitions possible from this energy level to ground state
questions.solution:
$\underset{\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2\right)}{\mathrm{N}_1}=\underset{\mathrm{KMnOO}_4 / \mathrm{H}^{+}}{\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{~V}_2}$
$2 \times 25=N \times 25$
$N=2$
Again $\underset{\substack{\mathrm{KMnO}_4 / \mathrm{H}^{+}}}{\mathrm{N}_1 \mathrm{~V}_1}=$ gm eq. of $(\mathrm{X})$
$\therefore 2 \times 1=$ gm eq. of $(X)$
and n factor of $\mathrm{x}=2$
Since X is converted into single e species Hence X is $\mathrm{Li}^{2+}$ and light radiation of $114 Å$ corresponds to 108.77 eV .
$\therefore$ e is excited into $3^{\text {rd }}$ energy level and total 3 transitions are possible.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Total number of electron in phosphorus which have zero value of at least one quantum number.
questions.solution:
${ }_{15} \mathrm{P} \longrightarrow 1 \mathrm{~s}^2, 2 \mathrm{~s}^2, 2 \mathrm{p}^6, 3 \mathrm{~s}^2 3 \mathrm{p}^3$ For 's' $\ell=0, \mathrm{~m}=0, \mathrm{e}^{-}=6$ For ' p ', $\ell=1, \mathrm{~m}=\longrightarrow$

Total $\mathrm{e}^{-}=6+2+1=9$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
